criteria.name: Mermaid Diagram Validity
criteria.description: The C2 container diagram must be valid Mermaid syntax that renders without errors in Mermaid Live Editor, using proper container shapes, labels, and directional arrows. Must render correctly in both light and dark themes with WCAG 2.1 AA compliant color contrast ratios (minimum 4.5:1 for normal text).

criteria.name: C4 Level 2 Completeness
criteria.description: The diagram must include all major containers: API Gateway (OpenAI/Anthropic compatible), Orchestrator Service, Model Router, Local Model Instances (Qwen, Nemotron), Frontier API Adapter, Monitoring Service, Health Check Service, Administrative Interface, Persistent Storage (Consul/KV, Vault), and External System boundary markers. Each container must be labeled with technology stack, responsibility, and replication factor.

criteria.name: Relationship Accuracy
criteria.description: All container relationships must show correct data flow: API Gateway → Orchestrator → Model Router → (Local/Frontier Models), with monitoring/health checks exposed externally, and self-learning system receiving traces from Model Routers asynchronously. Must explicitly distinguish synchronous (solid lines) from asynchronous (dashed lines) relationships, and show bidirectional flows for health checks.

criteria.name: PRD and Technology Alignment
criteria.description: Diagram must reflect PRD-specified Nomad deployment topology, Consul/Vault integration points, Golang implementation labels, bifrost usage for message routing, and hardware telemetry integration points (vLLM/llama.cpp metrics) for VRAM-aware routing. Must show network segmentation between public-facing (API Gateway) and internal containers (Model Instances) with firewall boundaries.

criteria.name: Narrative Documentation Quality
criteria.description: Accompanying markdown must include exactly 150-250 words explaining each container's purpose, key interactions, deployment topology, and how the diagram supports the developer and operations journeys from the PRD. Word count must be explicitly stated at the end of the narrative section.

criteria.name: Extensibility Edge Case
criteria.description: Diagram must demonstrate horizontal scaling capability by showing Model Router connecting to a pool of identical model instances with load balancer notation. Must visually separate configuration artifacts (Nomad job specs, Consul config) from application code (Golang binaries). Adding a new LLM model must be shown as a configuration change (Nomad job update) without touching application containers or requiring code deployment.

criteria.name: Failure Handling Visibility
criteria.description: Diagram must show fallback paths: when a model instance fails, requests route to alternate models via the Model Router with circuit breaker pattern indicators. Health checks must show automatic rerouting with explicit <500ms timeout thresholds. Must include dead letter queue path for unrouteable requests after 3 retries.

criteria.name: Security and Cost Boundaries
criteria.description: Diagram must show security zones (DMZ for API Gateway, internal for compute), authentication flows (mTLS between internal services), and cost differentiation (local models vs frontier API costs). Must indicate VRAM utilization tiers (e.g., 24GB, 48GB) affecting routing decisions with explicit hardware boundaries.
